hepatotoxicity_thresholds.YELLOW: ALT 2-3x ULN (80-120 U/L) -- recheck in 2 weeks, monitor closely
hepatotoxicity_thresholds.RED: ALT > 3x ULN (>120 U/L) -- STOP fluoxetine, evaluate for DILI

qtc_thresholds.GREEN: QTc < 450 ms (male) or < 460 ms (female) -- no concern
qtc_thresholds.YELLOW: QTc 450-480 ms (male) or 460-480 ms (female) -- check electrolytes
qtc_thresholds.RED: QTc > 500 ms OR increase > 60 ms from baseline -- STOP fluoxetine

initiation.monitoring: CGM mandatory, check BG 4x/day minimum if no CGM

week_4_titration.monitoring: CGM; look for hypoglycemia patterns (<70 mg/dL episodes)

week_8_titration.monitoring: CGM; time-in-range should be >70%; <4% time below range

ongoing.note: Continue adjusting insulin based on CGM data. Target: time-in-range 70-180 mg/dL >70%, time below 70 <4%. If C-peptide rises (beta cell recovery), further insulin reduction needed. Monthly review of insulin doses for first 6 months.

thresholds.GREEN: Semaglutide tolerated, TIR >70%, time below range <4%, no severe hypos
thresholds.YELLOW: TIR 60-70% OR time below range 4-8% OR >2 hypos/week <70 mg/dL
thresholds.RED: Severe hypoglycemia (<54 mg/dL with symptoms) OR time below range >8% -- REDUCE semaglutide dose or STOP temporarily

fasting_protocol.title: Safe Fasting Protocol for T1DM Patients on the Protocol
fasting_protocol.preamble: Fasting in T1DM is the single most dangerous component of the protocol. However, it is also ESSENTIAL for autophagy-mediated viral clearance and beta cell regeneration. The following protocol makes fasting SAFE by providing specific insulin adjustments, monitoring intervals, and abort criteria.
pre_fast_checklist: CGM calibrated and functional (or blood glucose meter + ketone meter available)
Blood ketone meter available (fingerstick BHB, NOT urine ketones)
Basal insulin dose calculated (see adjustment table below)
No alcohol in prior 24 hours (alcohol inhibits gluconeogenesis)
No vigorous exercise planned during fast
Another person aware you are fasting (safety contact)
Phone charged with emergency contact programmed
Starting glucose 80-180 mg/dL (do NOT start fast if <80 or >300)
Starting BHB <0.6 mM (do NOT start fast if already in ketosis)

24h_weekly_fast.correction_insulin: If glucose >250: give 50% of normal correction dose. If glucose >300: BREAK FAST and give full correction.
24h_weekly_fast.on_semaglutide: Reduce basal to 70% (semaglutide suppresses glucagon further)

5day_fmd.basal_insulin: Reduce to 80-85% of normal (FMD provides ~800 kcal/day)
5day_fmd.bolus_insulin: Reduce by 50-60% (small FMD meals need less bolus)
5day_fmd.correction_insulin: Use normal correction factor (FMD is not zero-calorie)

monitoring_schedule_during_fast.hourly_0_to_4: BG every 2 hours (CGM acceptable), ketones at hour 0 and 4
monitoring_schedule_during_fast.hourly_4_to_12: BG per CGM continuous, ketones every 4 hours
monitoring_schedule_during_fast.hourly_12_to_24: BG per CGM continuous, ketones every 4 hours
monitoring_schedule_during_fast.hourly_24_plus: BG per CGM continuous, ketones every 2 hours (closer to DKA window)

immediate_abort: Any DKA symptoms: nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, fruity breath, Kussmaul breathing
Blood glucose >300 mg/dL with BHB >1.5 mM (DKA prodrome)
Feeling unwell, confused, or unable to self-monitor
abort_action: BREAK FAST immediately: eat 30-50g fast carbs (juice, glucose tablets)
If BG <54: 15g glucose, recheck in 15 min, repeat if needed (Rule of 15)
If BHB >3.0 with symptoms: GO TO EMERGENCY ROOM (do not try to manage at home)
If BHB >5.0 or pH <7.30 (if measured): CALL 911 -- this is DKA
Take full correction insulin dose for current glucose reading
Do NOT resume fast for at least 48 hours after an abort event

post_fast_refeeding.first_meal: Small, balanced meal (30-40g carbs, protein, fat). NOT a large carb load.
post_fast_refeeding.insulin: Give 70-80% of normal bolus for first meal (insulin sensitivity is higher post-fast)
post_fast_refeeding.monitoring: Check BG 1h and 2h post-meal (rebound hyperglycemia is possible)
post_fast_refeeding.ketones: Check BHB 2h after refeeding (should be declining toward <0.6 mM)

renal_adjustment.gfr_below_30: AVOID colchicine -- accumulation risk too high

thresholds.GREEN: No GI symptoms, CBC normal, GFR >60 -- continue 0.5mg/day
thresholds.YELLOW: Mild diarrhea OR GFR 30-60 -- reduce to 0.25mg/day, monitor CBC
thresholds.RED: Persistent diarrhea + vomiting OR CBC abnormal (any cytopenia) -- STOP colchicine

parameters.name: Blood Glucose (fasting state)
parameters.action_yellow: Increase monitoring to every 30 min; prepare to break fast
parameters.action_red: BREAK FAST immediately; 15g fast carbs; recheck 15 min

parameters.name: Blood BHB (ketones)
parameters.action_yellow: Check every 2 hours; ensure adequate basal insulin; consider breaking fast
parameters.action_red: BREAK FAST; give correction insulin; if >5.0 or symptoms -> ER

parameters.name: Serum Bicarbonate
parameters.action_yellow: Check BHB and glucose; increase monitoring
parameters.action_red: Metabolic acidosis -- evaluate for DKA urgently

parameters.name: ALT (liver function)
parameters.action_yellow: Recheck in 2 weeks; consider fluoxetine hepatotoxicity
parameters.action_red: STOP fluoxetine; hepatology evaluation; check bilirubin (Hy's Law)

parameters.name: QTc (ECG)
parameters.action_yellow: Check K+, Mg2+; repeat ECG in 1 month; avoid other QTc drugs
parameters.action_red: STOP fluoxetine; cardiology evaluation; check electrolytes STAT

parameters.name: CBC (on colchicine)
parameters.red: WBC <3000 or Plt <100K or any cytopenia
parameters.action_yellow: Recheck in 2 weeks; consider dose reduction

parameters.name: Hypoglycemia Events
parameters.red: >3 per week or ANY severe (needing assistance)
parameters.action_yellow: Reduce insulin; increase monitoring; review semaglutide/fasting timing
parameters.action_red: STOP fasting; reduce semaglutide; endocrinology review

parameters.name: GI Symptoms (on colchicine)
parameters.yellow: Persistent diarrhea (>3 stools/day) or daily nausea
parameters.action_yellow: Reduce colchicine to 0.25mg/day; check renal function
parameters.action_red: STOP colchicine; check CBC (bone marrow toxicity); IV fluids if dehydrated

parameters.name: Weight (on semaglutide)
parameters.action_yellow: Reduce semaglutide dose; increase caloric intake on non-fast days
parameters.action_red: STOP semaglutide; nutrition assessment; may need to pause fasting too

absolute.condition: Recurrent DKA (>2 episodes in past year)
absolute.reason: Fasting component is too dangerous; risk of DKA is unacceptably high
absolute.alternative: Can do fluoxetine + supplements WITHOUT fasting. Reduced efficacy but safer.

absolute.reason: Fasting + SSRI (blunts hypo symptoms further) = severe hypoglycemia risk
absolute.alternative: Must restore hypo awareness FIRST (CGM education, HAATT). Then consider.

absolute.reason: Colchicine accumulation risk -- cannot use at any dose
absolute.alternative: Use full protocol WITHOUT colchicine. BHB from fasting provides NLRP3 inhibition.

absolute.reason: Fluoxetine causes mild QTc prolongation; additive risk with congenital LQTS
absolute.alternative: Consider another SSRI with less QTc effect, or non-SSRI antiviral if one exists.
Must washout MAOI x 2 weeks (or 5 weeks for fluoxetine washout if switching FROM).

absolute.reason: Fluoxetine: FDA category C; fasting: not recommended in pregnancy; semaglutide: contraindicated
absolute.alternative: Defer protocol until not pregnant and not planning pregnancy.

absolute.reason: Fasting component + semaglutide (appetite suppression) could exacerbate disorder
absolute.alternative: Treat eating disorder first. May use fluoxetine + supplements only (no fasting).

relative.reason: Fluoxetine: FDA-approved for pediatric depression; fasting in pediatric T1DM not well-studied
relative.management: Pediatric endocrinology supervision mandatory. Modified FMD only (not full fasting).

relative.reason: Fluoxetine clearance reduced; colchicine clearance reduced
relative.management: Reduce fluoxetine to 10mg. Avoid colchicine. Monitor ALT monthly.

relative.reason: Fasting may cause hemodynamic instability; electrolyte shifts dangerous
relative.management: No full fasting. Modified FMD only (800+ kcal/day). Cardiology co-management.

relative.reason: Fasting + electrolyte changes can lower seizure threshold
relative.management: Modified FMD only. Ensure adequate electrolyte supplementation during fasting.

relative.reason: Semaglutide worsens gastroparesis (further delays gastric emptying)
relative.management: Avoid semaglutide. Use remaining protocol components.

relative.reason: Fasting + semaglutide cause further weight loss
relative.management: Avoid semaglutide. Limit fasting to 24h only (not 5-day FMD). Caloric supplements.
